_source: Role split and target counts derived from the user's guide: docs/battle_brothers_guide_2026.md. It frames a 12-man company as roughly 6 frontline + 6 backline, and in 'Building specialists' gives per-role counts: frontline shield tanks 2-4, two-handed damage 2-3, polearm/reach line 2-3, archers/ranged 1-3, and one banner/sergeant.
_note: Targets are per ROLE, since the guide gives role-level counts rather than a target for each specific build. The UI shows your actual archetype breakdown inside each role, so you can still spot e.g. too many shield users. The guide's frontline (shield tanks + two-handers) maps onto the 'frontline' role group here; its polearm/reach line maps onto 'backline_melee'.

presets.name: Standard 12 (6 front / 6 back)
presets.note: The guide's common company: ~6 frontline (a mix of shield tanks and two-handers) and 6 backline made of a polearm/reach line, ranged, and a banner sergeant.

presets.name: Shield-and-reach wall (12)
presets.note: Leans on the guide's most-defensive picks: a fuller shield frontline with a strong polearm/reach line striking over the top. Fewer two-handers, safer against orcs and cavalry.

presets.name: Ranged-heavy (12)
presets.note: Toward the top of the guide's ranged range (archers/ranged up to 3): thin enemies before contact, backed by a solid frontline to hold while they fire.

presets.name: Early company (8)
presets.note: A smaller early-game band before you can afford specialists: mostly cheap frontline bodies with a couple of ranged, no dedicated banner yet.

presets.name: Heavy 2H Doomstack (12)
presets.note: The user's specific heavy-2H company plan. Table counts (using a single number within each range): 3 mace/axe frontliners, 2 hammer flankers, 1 cleaver/whip, 2 flank tanks, 1 backline sergeant, 3 thrower/polearm hybrids. Per-archetype targets are shown under each role.
